---
title: "Instrumental Variables"
subtitle: "A Short Introduction"
format:
  dtslides-revealjs:
    slide-number: c/t
    chalkboard: true
bibliography: refs.bib
author:
  - name: Subham Kailthya
    orcid: 0000-0002-6961-2502
    email: kailthya.subham@gmail.com
    affiliations: 
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Setting {.small-slide}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}
- Suppose $\beta_1$ is the [true]{.alert} causal effect of $X_{i}$ on $Y_{i}$ in the linear regression:
$$
Y_{i} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{i} + \varepsilon_{i}, \quad i=1, \ldots, N
$$
- For the least squares estimate $\hat{\beta}_{1}^{OLS}$ to be consistent for $\beta_1$ requires:
$$
\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon_i|X_i] = 0
$$
$\implies$ $X_i$ is **exogenous** and contains no additional information on $\varepsilon_i$.
- When $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon_i|X_i] \ne 0$, $X_i$ is **endogenous** and $\hat{\beta}_{1}^{OLS}$ is inconsistent for $\beta_{1}$.
- Example: Effect of `EDUC` on `LWKLYWGE` [@angrist1991does], where $\text{Cov(X, U)}>0$
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
[Example]{.highlight .center}
```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart TD
    X["X: EDUC"] -->|Causal effect| Y["Y: LWKLYWGE"]
    
    U["U: Ability"] --> X
    U --> Y
```
:::

::::

## IV: Basic idea {.small-slide}

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart TD
    Z["Z: Instrument"] -->|Relevance| X["X: Treatment"]
    X -->|Causal effect| Y["Y: Outcome"]
    
    U["U: Unobserved"] --> X
    U --> Y
    
    Z -.->|No direct effect - Exclusion| Y
```

## References {.tiny-slide}
